source_text: 19. Tabbing a Correspondence Package. Tab the signature page (if not the first page), enclosures, and background material. Label the tabs as appropriate. Figure 7-10 illustrates an assembled correspondence package with tabs. Take care so tabs can be removed without defacing the document.
plain_language_rule: Tab the signature page (unless it is the first page), all enclosures, and all background material in a correspondence package. Label each tab as appropriate. Ensure tabs can be removed without defacing any document. Figure 7-10 shows tab placement/usage for a correspondence package. Signature page, enclosures, and background material are tabbed as appropriate. Tabs must be removable without defacing the document. Figure 7-10 supports physical package preparation, not digital rendering.
notes: Figure 7-10 is rule-bearing but advisory/procedural. Do not convert figure tab placement into a hard pixel/validator rule unless later manual text explicitly requires it. Preserve the manual requirement that tabs can be removed without defacing the document. No renderer behavior is implied.
